checks.a_analysis: The output JSON faithfully represents the text and structure of the source PDF. I have verified each section against its corresponding page in the source document. No fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or headings were found. All descriptive text, such as image alt text, is appropriately identified as AI-generated.

The source document contains no phone numbers, email addresses, or mailing addresses. The term 'Microsoft 365' appears, which is correctly reflected in the output. No URLs or other contact information were fabricated.
All 25 pages of the original slide presentation are accounted for in the JSON output. Each content slide and title slide has been successfully converted into its respective section or element.

There is one table-like structure on page 14 (Best Practices) which was converted into a 2x2 table in the JSON. This structure correctly captures the four distinct points presented in the source document's grid layout. No data was lost.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: I performed a line-by-line comparison of several key content pages (e.g., pages 4, 13, 17, 21). The JSON output contains the full text of these slides without any significant omissions. Minor punctuation and whitespace adjustments are within acceptable parameters.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the JSON matches the source document's meaning and content. Some minor hyphenation changes (e.g., 'Work-in-Progress' on slide 13) were made, but these improve readability and do not alter the information provided.

checks.a_analysis: The conversion result was reviewed for any instances of duplicated text blocks or sections. Each slide's content from the source PDF appears exactly once in the corresponding section of the JSON output. There are no repeated paragraphs or list items that are not present in the original source.

The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent throughout the document. The main document title is appropriately captured, and subsequent slides are treated as major sections. Within those sections, sub-points (such as the specific features on Slides 4, 5, and 7-11) are assigned H3 levels, creating a clear and navigable hierarchy for screen readers without any skipped levels.

The grid-based layout on Slide 14 ('Best Practices') has been converted into a 2x2 table in the JSON. This structure accurately reflects the visual organization of the source PDF, where four distinct best practice items are arranged in a quadrant. The cell contents and layout (rows and columns) match the logical grouping of the original document.

The reading order in the JSON follows the sequential order of the slides in the source PDF from start to finish. The content within individual slides (such as headings followed by their respective paragraphs and images) also maintains the intended logical flow of information.

The agenda items on Slide 2 are correctly identified and converted as an unordered list in the JSON. Other content throughout the document that appeared as individual blocks (e.g., Use Cases on Slide 13) has been correctly structured using headings and paragraphs, which preserves the meaning and emphasis of the original layout.
The source PDF does not contain any clickable URLs or contact links (except for a QR code). The JSON output correctly reflects this by not injecting any fabricated or erroneous links.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output successfully translates the presentation slide format into a linear, web-friendly document. The visual organization of information remains consistent with the source PDF, following the logical order of slides and headings. Changes in font and layout are expected for responsive HTML.
The source PDF does not contain any formal data tables. Content presented in grid-like layouts (e.g., slides 13, 14, 16, 17) has been rendered appropriately as styled text blocks in the HTML.

Several meaningful images from the source PDF are missing in the HTML output and have been replaced by solid green rectangular placeholders. This occurs specifically on content derived from Slide 8 (Collaboration) and Slide 21 (Power Automate Flows). The images for 'OneDrive Co-authoring', 'Advanced SharePoint Features', 'Automating Approvals', and 'Custom App Development' are all affected.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 8 and Page 21 of the PDF contain descriptive graphics for collaboration and automation features (e.g., laptop icons, node diagrams, cog wheels, and building blocks).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows solid green boxes in the 'Collaboration' and 'Power Automate Flows' sections where the graphics should be.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 'Collaboration' and 'Integration Scenarios > Power Automate Flows'

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of body content (paragraphs, images, or sections) being duplicated in the HTML output. Section headings appear in the navigation/TOC and the body, which is a standard accessibility feature.
